briefing: NASDAQ fell 0.7% and S&P 500 slipped 0.2%, confirming a neutral-to-weak day. Per established strategy, neutral days are hold days — cash of $577.14 sits just above the $500 threshold but deploying into new positions without a risk-on signal would be forcing trades. Existing six positions remain intact; stop-losses and profit targets continue working automatically.

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Risk-off describes a market mood where investors pull back from higher-risk assets like growth stocks and move toward safety. On days like today — where major indices dip and momentum is absent — disciplined investors conserve cash rather than chase positions, waiting for a confirmed risk-on signal before deploying capital.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「Risk-Off（避險情緒）」是指市場參與者傾向從高風險資產（如成長股）撤退，轉向較安全資產的市場氛圍。在類似今日這種指數小幅下跌、動能缺失的環境下，紀律嚴明的投資者會保留現金而非追高建倉，等待明確的「Risk-On（風險偏好回升）」訊號後再行部署資金。

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities drifted lower on July 1 as investors paused after a strong June rally, with profit-taking weighing on tech names and the NASDAQ underperforming the broader index. Thin holiday-week volume amplified small moves, leaving the S&P 500 roughly flat while growth stocks gave back modest gains. Markets appeared to be in a consolidation phase, with traders awaiting fresh catalysts — including upcoming jobs data and Fed commentary — before extending exposure.
market_education.summary_zh: 7月1日美股整體走低，NASDAQ下跌0.7%，標普500小跌0.2%，市場在6月強勢反彈後出現獲利回吐。假期週前後交投清淡，成交量偏低令個別波動被放大，科技股受壓相對明顯。市場進入盤整格局，投資者靜候非農就業數據及聯儲局官員言論等新催化劑，暫不急於加碼。
